summary: Iter9 — P0 unification wave (universal hooks + Hermes unification + Document Parsing) verified. New /app/backend/tests/test_p0_unification.py: 13/13 PASS. Plus key regressions from backend_test.py (voice/converse, chat/stream SSE, roi/current) still PASS. Covers: (1) /api/voice/converse, (2) /api/hermes/chat, (3) /api/hermes/ultra, (4) /api/personas/hermes/chat, (5) /api/chat/stream done frame all now return request_id+confidence_level+should_escalate. (6) /api/requests shows audit rows from web, stream, voice, hermes_chat, hermes_ultra, persona, documents channels. (7) /api/roi/current.by_agent_cost contains hermes_coo, hermes_ultra, persona:*, voice. (8) Hermes shims (hermes_coo.enhanced_chat, hermes_max_tools_and_coo.HERMES_TOOLS) re-export canonical agents.hermes; HERMES_TOOLS has all 10 core + 5 stub tools. (9) create_followup tool-call lands in db.tasks with kind='followup' (legacy db.followups not written to). (10-12) /api/documents/upload returns full schema incl. request_id/chunks/mempalace_stored_chunks, doc listed via GET /documents and GET /documents/{id}, and mempalace_search wing=documents finds the uploaded chunks. (13) /api/personas/compliance/chat (plan=enterprise) used mempalace_search and/or referenced the document in content.

minor.issue: First run returned 502 'preview environment is not responding' (ingress timeout on cold-start); retried immediately → PASS in 7.6s. Flaky preview-ingress behaviour, NOT a code regression.

critical_code_review_comments: agents/_pipeline_hooks.finalize_llm_turn is a clean single-responsibility hook: cost → reliability → escalation → audit row → return payload. Best-effort try/except around each side-effect block prevents hook failures from breaking LLM responses — good defensive design.

agents/documents.ingest_document chunks at fixed 6KB byte boundaries (`text[i:i+CHUNK_CHARS]`), which can split mid-codepoint for multi-byte UTF-8 and mid-sentence for semantic search quality. Acceptable for v1 (TXT/PDF/DOCX with mostly ASCII/Cyrillic 2-byte), but consider tiktoken-aware or sentence-boundary chunker before scaling to large policy PDFs.
agents/documents.analyse_risk caps context at text[:18000] chars — silently truncates large documents. Should surface `truncated: true` flag in the verdict for client awareness.

server.py /api/personas/{persona_id}/chat tariff-gate condition (line ~1287) has operator-precedence bug: `not result.get('success') and 'не доступна' in (...) or (not result.get('success') and 'недоступна' in ...)`. Python evaluates `and` before `or` — works by accident here because both branches share the same first conjunct, but the expression is hard to read and brittle. Recommend rewriting as: `if not result.get('success') and any(k in (result.get('error') or '').lower() for k in ('не доступна','недоступна')):`.

agents/orchestrator.list_recent_requests has no channel/filter param — once `db.requests` grows past ~10k rows, /api/requests will start to lag without an index on `created_at` (descending) or channel. core/db.py should add `requests` index (created_at:-1) and (channel:1, created_at:-1).

agents/hermes.py is 527 lines — at the edge of the 700-line guideline. As more tools land (especially the 5 mock-stub fill-ins), split tool implementations into agents/hermes_tools.py and keep agents/hermes.py for the orchestration loop only.

voice/tts endpoint returns raw audio/mpeg (Content-Type: audio/mpeg) but other voice endpoints (voice/converse, voice/stt) return JSON envelopes. This is an API-shape inconsistency. Either always return JSON with audio_b64, or expose /api/voice/tts/raw + /api/voice/tts/json variants. The old test_voice_tts_returns_mp3 (line ~279 of backend_test.py) likely already handles this — verified other tests work — but it can trip up new SDK consumers.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_p0_unification.py (new file — 13 tests covering all P0 review_request items)
test_credentials: n/a — all endpoints open per main agent
seed_data_creation: Tests insert TEST_-prefixed data: db.requests audit rows across 7 channels, db.tasks kind=followup rows tagged TEST_FU_<hex>, db.documents row 'TEST contract upload' with MemPalace wing=documents room=<doc_id> chunks. No teardown — backend exposes no delete API for these collections; cleanup expected via manual collection prune or test-data-prune script if pollution becomes an issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 wave (Phase 1 universal hooks + Phase 3 Hermes unification + should_escalate fix + Document Parsing) is fully working in this fork. Run regression: pytest backend/tests/test_p0_unification.py -v (≈90s, all DeepSeek-live). If /api/hermes/ultra returns 502, retry immediately — it is a preview-ingress cold-start flake, not a code bug. Voice/TTS endpoint returns raw audio/mpeg bytes (not JSON envelope) — fixture in test_p0_unification.py handles both content-types. 5 Hermes tools are still MOCKED (generate_communication_summary, suggest_next_best_action, find_opportunities_in_contact, suggest_reply_template, evaluate_action_roi) — that is documented and expected, do NOT flag as a regression.
rca of the issue: n/a — no live bugs. Initial test-side issues during authoring: (a) /api/voice/tts content-type was assumed to be JSON, but the endpoint returns raw audio/mpeg — fixture updated to handle both; (b) followup-collection test imported get_db() before /app/backend/.env was loaded into the pytest process — added dotenv load + new event loop. Both fixes are test-only, no backend changes.
